maoReminder: Our MAO stays ARV × 90% − (Repairs × 2). Fund buy-box % notes are exit intel only — never replace our underwrite.

funds.name: Siren Fund
funds.oneLiner: Brick DFW house, good neighborhood, 1970s+, $30–60k rehab, ARV $300–400k → Siren-shaped.
funds.type: Active Dallas hedge fund · ~2 homes/week in strong DFW suburbs
funds.strategy: Clean brick SFH · predictable rehab · strong rental demand
funds.pitchNotes: Typically ~75%–78% of ARV minus repairs (exit intel). Target rent $2,000+.

funds.name: Odin Fund
funds.oneLiner: Clean light–med flip with strong comps in Monroe County → Odin-shaped.
funds.buyerStyle: Cash · fast · sight-unseen OK if pics are solid
funds.pitchNotes: Not overly price-sensitive when comps support. Ideal 1950s–1980s builds.

funds.name: Neptune Fund
funds.oneLiner: Ohio SFH, light rehab, under $250k — especially with seller open to leaseback → Neptune-shaped.
funds.pitchNotes: Pitch 1–3 yr seller stay at below-market rent when open. Often better pricing + faster approvals on leasebacks. Solar must be paid off.

funds.name: Kraken Fund
funds.oneLiner: Off-market FL SFH in listed counties/JAX, ≤$250k, enough spread for 15–20% after costs → Kraken-shaped.
funds.strategy: Off-market discounted houses with real spread
funds.buyerStyle: Needs meat on the bone (not skinny deals)
funds.pitchNotes: Their formula ~75% ARV − Repairs (exit intel). Needs 15–20% profit after closing costs. Block homes 1960+; wood frame 1970+.

funds.name: Vulcan Fund
funds.oneLiner: Detroit/Tri-Cities with $200k+ ARV flip upside or livable Detroit under $35k light work → Vulcan-shaped.
funds.strategy: Ugly houses with upside; flips or cheap livable
funds.pitchNotes: Two boxes: higher-ARV flips, or cheap livable Detroit under $35k. Vacant preferred at close on flips.

funds.name: Atlas Fund
funds.oneLiner: Sac off-market fixer ≤$500k or SFV/LA/OC inner-tract headache house ≤$1.5M → Atlas-shaped.
funds.strategy: Off-market fixers where seller needs a clean cash exit
funds.buyerStyle: Often sight-unseen yes/no within hours if box fits
funds.pitchNotes: Two boxes: Sacramento ≤$500k, or SoCal inner-tract ≤$1.5M.

funds.name: Titan Fund
funds.oneLiner: SFH/duplex in Clayton or Macon-Bibb listed zips with clean title → Titan-shaped (fast closer).
funds.strategy: SFH and duplexes in working-class flip markets
funds.buyerStyle: No DD · $10k NR EMD · close when title ready
funds.pitchNotes: No due diligence period. $10,000 non-refundable EMD. Clean title path is critical.

buyBoxes.label: Clayton + Macon-Bibb
criteriaSummary: Clean title · $10k NR EMD · no DD

funds.name: Summit Fund
funds.oneLiner: Denver SFH, 3/2, ≤2,000 sqft — cash or creative, any condition → Summit-shaped.
funds.strategy: Simple residential deals; cash or creative finance
funds.pitchNotes: Flexible on condition and occupancy. Creative finance considered.

funds.name: Harbor Fund
funds.oneLiner: Lexington NC SFH, 3/2, 1,500+ sqft — waterfront is premium; cash or creative → Harbor-shaped.
funds.type: North Carolina SFH buyer focused on Lexington
funds.strategy: Normal residential demand; waterfront preferred but not required
funds.pitchNotes: Waterfront is a premium pitch, not a hard requirement. Don't skip good non-water deals.

funds.name: Gaia Fund
funds.oneLiner: Vacant lot or teardown in 75203 / 08 / 12 / 16 / 41, buildable, no flood → Gaia-shaped.
funds.type: Dallas-based fund · infill land and teardown houses
funds.strategy: Feed builder / infill demand in hot Dallas zips
funds.buyerStyle: Cash · deep discount · can move same day
funds.pitchNotes: Target contract around $100k when possible. Typical lot value $150–200k+. Common assignment $15–30k+.

funds.name: Leviathan Fund
funds.oneLiner: Off-market FL vacant land, res/MF zoning, 0.15–5 ac, road + address, ≤$300k, ~30%+ under market → Leviathan-shaped.
funds.type: Florida cash buyer · vacant residential / multifamily land
funds.buyerStyle: Cash · 5–10 business day close when box fits
funds.pitchNotes: Typically 30%+ below market. Must have road access and an address.

funds.name: Blackfin Fund
funds.oneLiner: Buildable South FL residential infill for SFH/duplex, utils nearby preferred → Blackfin-shaped.
funds.type: South Florida land buyer · residential infill
funds.strategy: Buildable lots for SFH or duplex builders
funds.buyerStyle: Any lot size · utilities nearby preferred
funds.pitchNotes: Best near existing development, utilities, roads, and active new construction.
